chunk_id: docling_book_en_国外数学教材-数论-Melvyn B. Nathanson—Elementary Methods in Number Theory_0242_11
text: An ordered factorization of the positive integer n into exactly l factors is an l-tuple (d1,..., de) such that n = dj... de. The divisor function d(n) counts the number of ordered factorizations of n into exactly two factors, since each factorization n = dd' is completely determined by the first factor d. For every positive integer l, we define the arithmetic function den) as the number of factorizations of n into exactly l factors. Then di (n) = 1 and da(n) = d(n) for all n.
page: book_en_国外数学教材-数论-Melvyn B. Nathanson—Elementary Methods in Number Theory_0242
